title: Myroxylon pringlei
intro: Myroxylon pringlei, also known as Pringle's Palo Santo, is a small tree native to Central America, belonging to the family Fabaceae. This plant is prized for its aromatic wood and resin, which possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable natural remedy for wound healing, skin issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Myroxylon pringlei include sesquiterpenes, specifically isoboldine and palo santo, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Myroxylon pringlei is commonly used to make essential oils, extracts, and incense sticks, which are used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve spiritual well-being.

This species is native to Mexico and is a member of the genus Myroxylon, which contains several other species of trees.
